title: Reproducible env + pipeline skeleton (pinned, deterministic)
context: M1 pilot. A pinned, deterministic environment and pipeline skeleton so every downstream dataset run is reproducible by an independent runner. Binding cancer guardrails apply: ONLY open-access / aggregate / de-identified public data; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; not medical advice; when in doubt, stop and surface. No partner/beneficiary is secured yet, so verifiedNeed is false.
objective: Build a reproducible, pinned environment and a deterministic pipeline skeleton that downstream standardize/annotate/integrate steps plug into.
acceptanceCriteria: Environment is fully pinned (lockfile / container) and runs deterministically.
Pipeline skeleton has stages for acquire, standardize, annotate, integrate, release with provenance hooks.
Clean-machine run reproduces outputs; CI exercises the skeleton.
No secrets or large data binaries committed; commit signed off per DCO.
output: A pull request adding the pinned environment and deterministic pipeline skeleton.
